We’re thrilled to share the shortlisted films for The Wonder Film Festival.

The level of talent has been extraordinary, we were genuinely blown away by the creativity and imagination behind every submission.

Our 30 shortlisted films have been grouped into five categories and will now move on to judging by our incredible jury to select the 3 winners.
Mati Staniszewski
🌟Rourke Sefton-Minns
Justin Hackney
Justine Moore
Danny Perkins
The categories are:
• Animation
• Drama
• Experimental
• Horror
• Narrative

The filmmakers 👇🏼

ANIMATION
Edmond Yang 🚀
- The Granniest Grandma
Gabriel Horn
-  ISMÁLIA
Noah Wagner
- A Fistful of Feathers
Jose Alvarez
- Finch
Louise Giboulot-Squarcioni
- Funeral Crasher
Herinario Rakotomanana - More Tears Than Harm
Trisha Code - Pinkington: Owlvolution

DRAMA
Javier De la Chica Soriano
- Ausencia
Mehdi Saqi
- The Prompt Floor
Elena Savlokhova
- Xi
Ikenna Mokwe ⭐️
- The Late 1900’s
Jiri Kilevnik
- Mr A List

EXPERIMENTAL
Andres Aloi
- Carousel
Jordan Daniel Chesney
- 91
Mark Wachholz
- The Cinema That Never Was
Martin Haerlin
- Grandmere
Simon Rieth - 6000 Lies
Uisato- KRAR
Yalin Ozgencil, PhD
- Who Asked Me To Remember

HORROR
Danny Tseng - TORII
BLUDSIMPLE - Teeth
Nicolas Pomet
- The Russian Sleep Experiment
Tedra Wilson
- Mud
The Garra Sisters - Morphé

NARRATIVE
Alex Naghavi
- Railbound
Forevercoast- Erase
Frank Bell- Verité
Frankie Caradonna
- THE LAST IMAGE
Jacob Adler - Rules of Play
METACIZ - Cosmic Joke

Check out the full films inside The Wonder App - https://
app.wonderstudios.com

Jose Alvarez
5mo
Report this comment
Today I sat and watched all the shortlisted nominees and I have to say that being among you is genuinely inspiring. I never knew where this path would take me and to find myself in the same category as so many incredible creators is unbelievable. This journey has been a mix of doubt and courage, putting my ideas, my vision, and my effort on the line without knowing how they would be received. It’s never easy to shape a feeling, a moment, or a vision into a short film, especially when the people around you don’t always understand how you think or create, but I’ve always loved film from childhood to now, the directors, the actors, the cinematography, the craft of it all. After my accident four years ago when I lost the ability to make music and perform, I had no idea where my creativity would go. Falling into the AI filmmaking world gave me a new outlet, a new spark, a way to build stories without cameras, sets, actors, or lighting. Watching all your films today, the colour, the tone, the aesthetics, the sound, the emotion, I’m honestly blown away. I’m humbled, grateful, and deeply honoured to stand alongside you in this festival. Thank you for the opportunity.
